\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Simple}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.Hypercontractivity.Simple
\section{Overview}

This module develops the ``simple'' hypercontractivity results for real-valued
Boolean functions $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$, that is, the results obtainable by
direct induction on coordinates without the full general machinery. It builds up
the $(2,4)$, $(4/3,2)$, $(2,2k)$, and $(p,2)$ hypercontractivity inequalities for
the noise operator $T_\rho$, together with the supporting character, cardinality,
Fourier, and combinatorial lemmas used along the way.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{lemma}[Character of a lifted set ignores the last coordinate]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.chiS_snoc_castSucc}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.chiS}
For a subset $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x \in \{0,1\}^n$, and a bit $b$,
the parity character of the lifted set equals the character of $S$ on $x$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b) = \chi_S(x).
\]
That is, appending a last coordinate $b$ and not including it in the set leaves the
character unchanged.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Character of a lifted set with the last coordinate]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.chiS_snoc_with_last}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.boolToSign, BooleanAnalysis.chiS}
For $S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n$, a point $x$, and a bit $b$, adjoining the last
coordinate to the lifted set multiplies the character by the sign of $b$:
\[
  \chi_{\,\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}}(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = (-1)^{b}\,\chi_S(x),
\]
where $(-1)^b$ denotes \lean{boolToSign}\,$b$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Partition of a sum over subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.finset_fin_succ_sum_partition}
\leanok
For any $\varphi$ on subsets of $\mathrm{Fin}(n+1)$, the total sum splits according to
whether the last coordinate belongs to the subset:
\[
  \sum_{S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}(n+1)} \varphi(S)
  = \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T))
  + \sum_{T \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \varphi(\mathrm{castSucc}(T)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}).
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cardinality of a lifted set]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.card_image_castSucc}
\leanok
Lifting a set along $\mathrm{castSucc}$ preserves its cardinality:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)| = |S|$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Cardinality of a lifted set with the last coordinate]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.card_image_castSucc_union_last}
\leanok
Adjoining the (necessarily new) last coordinate to a lifted set increases its
cardinality by one:
$|\mathrm{castSucc}(S)\cup\{\mathrm{last}\,n\}| = |S| + 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Noise operator decomposes along the last coordinate]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.noiseOp_snoc}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.fourierCoeff_avgLast, Bonami.fourierCoeff_diffLast, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.boolToSign, BooleanAnalysis.chiS, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.card_image_castSucc, SimpleHypercontractivity.card_image_castSucc_union_last, SimpleHypercontractivity.chiS_snoc_castSucc, SimpleHypercontractivity.chiS_snoc_with_last, SimpleHypercontractivity.finset_fin_succ_sum_partition}
For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$ the noise operator splits over the last coordinate:
\[
  T_\rho f(\mathrm{snoc}\,x\,b)
  = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)(x) + (-1)^b\,\rho\,T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)(x),
\]
where $\mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and $\mathrm{diffLast}\,f$ are the average and difference of
$f$ over the last bit.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Fourth moment decomposition with the noise operator]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.fourth_moment_noise_decomp}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.fourth_moment_decomp, Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.noiseOp_snoc}
For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the fourth moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^4]
  = \E[(T_\rho g)^4] + 6\rho^2\,\E[(T_\rho g)^2 (T_\rho h)^2] + \rho^4\,\E[(T_\rho h)^4].
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Key algebraic recurrence inequality]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_algebra'}
\leanok
Suppose $a,b \ge 0$, $B \ge 0$, $A \le a^2$, $B \le b^2$, $C^2 \le AB$, and
$\rho^2 \le 1/3$. Then
\[
  A + 6\rho^2 C + \rho^4 B \le (a+b)^2.
\]
This is the algebraic step that closes the induction in the $(2,4)$ theorem.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,4)$-Hypercontractivity (Bonami--Beckner)]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_4}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.expect_cs_sq, Bonami.expect_fourth_nonneg, Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg, Bonami.second_moment_decomp, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight, SimpleHypercontractivity.fourth_moment_noise_decomp, SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_algebra'}
For any Boolean function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$ and noise parameter $\rho$ with
$\rho^2 \le 1/3$ (equivalently $\abs{\rho} \le 1/\sqrt3$),
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^4] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^2,
\]
that is $\norm{T_\rho f}_4 \le \norm{f}_2$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Hölder's inequality for $p = 4/3$, $q = 4$]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.innerProduct_le_L43_L4}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For Boolean functions $f, g$,
\[
  \langle f, g \rangle
  \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^{4/3}\,]\bigr)^{3/4}\,\bigl(\E[\,\abs{g}^{4}\,]\bigr)^{1/4}.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[$(4/3,2)$-Hypercontractivity with $\rho = 1/\sqrt3$]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_4_div_3_2}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_self_adjoint, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight, SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_4, SimpleHypercontractivity.innerProduct_le_L43_L4}
Boolean functions are $(4/3, 2)$-hypercontractive with parameter $1/\sqrt3$: for any
$f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$,
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_{1/\sqrt3} f)^2]\bigr)^{1/2}
  \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^{4/3}\,]\bigr)^{3/4}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[$L^2$ norm of $T_\rho f$ in Fourier space]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.noise_l2_fourier}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.fourierCoeff, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_fourier, BooleanAnalysis.parseval}
The squared $L^2$ norm of $T_\rho f$ expands over the Fourier spectrum:
\[
  \langle T_\rho f, T_\rho f \rangle
  = \sum_{S \subseteq \mathrm{Fin}\,n} \rho^{2\abs{S}}\,\widehat{f}(S)^2.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Contractivity ($q = 2$ case)]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.contractivity}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.parseval, SimpleHypercontractivity.noise_l2_fourier}
For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \E[f^2].
\]
This is the $q = 2$ case of hypercontractivity.
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Binomial coefficient bound]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.binom_coeff_ineq}
\leanok
For natural numbers $k \ge 1$ and $j \le k$,
\[
  \binom{2k}{2j} \le \binom{k}{j}\,(2k-1)^j.
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$q$-th moment decomposition along the last coordinate]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.qth_moment_decomp}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.sum_boolCube_succ, Bonami.uniformWeight_succ, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect}
For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = \mathrm{avgLast}\,f$ and
$h = \mathrm{diffLast}\,f$, the $q$-th moment decomposes as
\[
  \E[f^q] = \E\!\left[\frac{(g+h)^q + (g-h)^q}{2}\right].
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$q$-th moment decomposition with the noise operator]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.noise_qth_moment_decomp}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.restrictLast, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.boolToSign, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.noiseOp_snoc, SimpleHypercontractivity.qth_moment_decomp}
For $f : \{0,1\}^{n+1} \to \bbr$, writing $g = T_\rho(\mathrm{avgLast}\,f)$ and
$h = T_\rho(\mathrm{diffLast}\,f)$, the $q$-th moment of $T_\rho f$ decomposes as
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^q]
  = \E\!\left[\frac{(g + \rho h)^q + (g - \rho h)^q}{2}\right].
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,2k)$-Hypercontractivity (Bonami--Beckner)]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_2k}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.avgLast, Bonami.diffLast, Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg, Bonami.second_moment_decomp, BooleanAnalysis.BoolCube, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight, BooleanAnalysis.walsh_expansion, SimpleHypercontractivity.binom_coeff_ineq, SimpleHypercontractivity.noise_qth_moment_decomp}
For any Boolean function $f : \{0,1\}^n \to \bbr$, integer $k \ge 1$, and noise
parameter $\rho$ with $\rho^2 \le 1/(2k-1)$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^{2k}] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^k,
\]
equivalently $\norm{T_\rho f}_{2k} \le \norm{f}_2$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,q)$-Hypercontractivity for even $q$]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_q}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_2k}
For an even integer $q \ge 2$ and noise parameter $\rho$ with $\rho^2 \le 1/(q-1)$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^q] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^{q/2}.
\]
This is the $(2,2k)$ theorem restated in terms of an even exponent $q = 2k$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,2)$-Hypercontractivity]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_2}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.contractivity}
For $\rho^2 \le 1$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^2] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^1,
\]
the $q = 2$ instance, which is just contractivity.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,6)$-Hypercontractivity]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_6}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_q}
For $\rho^2 \le 1/5$ and any Boolean function $f$,
\[
  \E[(T_\rho f)^6] \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^3.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[$(2,2k)$-Hypercontractivity in norm form]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_2k_rpow}
\leanok
\uses{Bonami.expect_sq_nonneg, BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_2_2k}
For $k \ge 1$ and noise parameter $\rho$ with $\rho^2 \le 1/(2k-1)$, the $(2,2k)$
bound in the form $\norm{T_\rho f}_{2k} \le \norm{f}_2$:
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_\rho f)^{2k}]\bigr)^{1/(2k)} \le \bigl(\E[f^2]\bigr)^{1/2}.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{lemma}[Inner product equals expected square]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.innerProduct_eq_expect_sq}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
The self inner product of a Boolean function equals the expectation of its square:
$\langle f, f \rangle = \E[f^2]$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Nonnegativity of the expected square of $T_\rho f$]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.expect_sq_noiseOp_nonneg}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For any $\rho$ and Boolean function $f$, $\E[(T_\rho f)^2] \ge 0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Nonnegativity of an expected absolute power]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.expect_rpow_abs_nonneg}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.uniformWeight}
For any real exponent $p$ and Boolean function $f$, $\E[\,\abs{f}^p\,] \ge 0$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Composition of noise operators]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.noiseOp_compose}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_fourier}
Composing two noise operators multiplies their parameters:
$T_\rho(T_\sigma f) = T_{\rho\sigma} f$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[$(p,2)$-Hypercontractivity via duality]
\lean{SimpleHypercontractivity.hypercontractivity_p_2_general}
\leanok
\uses{BooleanAnalysis.BooleanFunc, BooleanAnalysis.expect, BooleanAnalysis.innerProduct, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp, BooleanAnalysis.noiseOp_self_adjoint, SimpleHypercontractivity.expect_rpow_abs_nonneg, SimpleHypercontractivity.expect_sq_noiseOp_nonneg, SimpleHypercontractivity.innerProduct_eq_expect_sq}
Let $1 < p$, $q \ge 2$ with $1/p + 1/q = 1$. Given a Hölder inequality for the pair
$(p,q)$ and a $(2,q)$-hypercontractivity bound for $T_\rho$, one concludes the dual
$(p,2)$ bound:
\[
  \bigl(\E[(T_\rho f)^2]\bigr)^{1/2} \le \bigl(\E[\,\abs{f}^p\,]\bigr)^{1/p}.
\]
\end{theorem}
